segment-text: INSIDE OUT original story by
Ronnie Del Carmen
Meg LeFauve Josh Cooley
JOY (V.O.) Do you ever look at someone and wonder, "What is going on inside
their head?" Well, I know. I know Riley's head.
INT. HOSPITAL - DAY
A newborn baby swaddled in a blanket, held by her parents. Push in... and ZOOM IN TO HER HEAD.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Out of the blackness steps a glowing figure. This is JOY. The room is black except for a bright CONSCIOUSNESS SCREEN. Hmm?
JOY
In front of Joy is a single large BUTTON. She pushes it.
INT. HOSPITAL - CONTINUOUS
The baby gurgles and wiggles happily.
And there she was...
INT. HEADQUARTERS - CONTINUOUS
ON THE CONSCIOUSNESS SCREEN:
Hello, Riley.
Oh look at you. Aren't you a little bundle of joy?
A GOLDEN GLOWING SPHERE rolls from behind the screen. It's a MEMORY of what we just saw: Mom and Dad cooing at Riley. Whoa.
INSIDE OUT
Joy rolls the memory on its track, illuminating the room. She turns back to the button and pushes it again.
INT. HOSPITAL - CONTINUOUS
Baby Riley gurgles happily.
It was amazing. Just Riley and me, forever...
INT. HEADQUARTERS - CONTINUOUS
Baby Riley CRIES.
...for 33 seconds.
Joy looks to her side. There's a new, droopy, blue character touching the button.
I'm Sadness.
Oh, hello. I'm Joy.
Joy tries to muscle past Sadness to press the button.
Can I just... if you could... I just want to fix that. Thanks.
The two struggle.
And that was just the beginning. Headquarters only got more crowded from there.
INT. MINNESOTA HOUSE - DAY
Riley, now age 3, barrels through the house pulling a wagon.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
ON THE SCREEN: Riley's POV as she runs through the house. Fear "supervises" Joy, who drives.
Very nice. Okay, looks like you got
this. Very good, sharp turn... Riley approaches a POWER CORD on the ground.
Ahh! Look out!!! No!
Fear leaps to the console.
That's Fear. He's really good at
INT. MINNESOTA HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
Riley stops. She cautiously steps over the power cord.
Easy... we're good! We're good.
A fear memory (purple) rolls in. JOY/SADNESS
Thank you! Thank you very much.
Joy jumps back into driving position.
And we're back!
INT. MINNESOTA HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
Riley continues her sprint through the house. Suddenly she's picked up.
INT. KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
PLOP into highchair. Bib. Tray. Bowl.
Here we go. Alright, open.
INSIDE OUT
Dad lifts a spoonful of food to Riley's mouth.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Hmm. This looks new.
FEAR/SADNESS
ON THE SCREEN: a spoonful of broccoli. DISGUST enters.
Okay, caution! There is a dangerous smell, people. Hold on, what is that?
This is Disgust. She basically
keeps Riley from being poisoned,
That is not brightly colored or shaped like a dinosaur... Hold on guys... It's broccoli!
Disgust GAGS and pulls a lever.
Yucky!
YOUNG RILEY Riley swats the broccoli. It flies into Dad's face.
A disgust memory (green) rolls in.
Well, I just saved our lives. Yeah. You're welcome.
INSIDE OUT
INT. KITCHEN
Riley, if you don't eat your dinner, you're not going to get any dessert.
ANGER looks up from reading his newspaper.
Wait. Did he just say we couldn't have dessert?
That's Anger. He cares very deeply about things being fair.
So that's how you want to play it, old man? No dessert? Oh, sure, we'll eat our dinner, right after YOU eat this! GrrrraaaAAHH!!!!
Anger takes the controls as FLAMES blast out of his head. An anger memory (red) rolls in.
INT. KITCHEN
Riley throws a fit. Dad distracts her.
('flying' the spoon) Hey, Riley, Riley, here comes an airplane!
Anger stops screaming.
Oh, airplane. We got an airplane, everybody. Ohh!/Ahh!
DISGUST/FEAR/SADNESS/JOY INT. KITCHEN
Dad "lands" the food into Riley's smiling mouth.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
The others leave Joy alone at the console with Sadness.
And you've met Sadness. She... well, she...
FLASH CUTS of Riley crying: broken toy, spilled ice cream, tantrum in the supermarket, wanting out of her car seat.
I'm not actually sure what she does. And I've checked, there's no place for her to go, so she's good, we're good. It's all great!
Sadness exits. Joy turns to the wall of golden memories.
Anyway! These are Riley's memories-and they're mostly happy, you'll notice, not to brag.
She inspects one of the golden memories: Riley and her friend MEG (both age 3) going down a slide. She REWINDS it, relishing the happy moment.
But the REALLY important ones are over here. I don't want to get too technical, but these are called CORE MEMORIES.
Joy pops up the CORE MEMORY HOLDER. Inside are five intensely bright golden memories.
Each one came from a superimportant time in Riley's life. Like when she first scored a goal? That was so amazing!
EXT. MINNESOTA LAKE, WINTER - DAY
Two-and-a-half-year-old Riley shoots a hockey puck across the ice. She trips and accidentally scores a goal.
MOM/DAD (cheering) Heeey! Would you look at that?! We got a future center here!
INT. HEADQUARTERS

A super-bright CORE MEMORY rolls in. It rolls to the Core Memory Holder. Clicking into position, a LIGHTLINE shoots out. The Emotions follow it to the back window and watch a new ISLAND OF PERSONALITY form.
And each core memory powers a different aspect of Riley's personality. Like Hockey Island! Five ISLANDS OF PERSONALITY float in space, miles from
Headquarters, connected by LIGHTLINES -- their power sources.
Goofball Island is my personal favorite.
INT. MINNESOTA LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
Toddler Riley runs naked from the bathroom, underpants on her head, laughing wildly. Dad chases her with a towel.
Come back here, you little monkey!
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Goofball Island activates: lights, movement, etc.
INT. MINNESOTA LIVING ROOM
Naked Riley does the goofy dance on the sofa.
Yup, Goofball is the best! Friendship Island is pretty good too.
Joy looks at FRIENDSHIP ISLAND.
INSIDE OUT EXT. SIDEWALK - DAY
Riley and Meg walk leg over leg, arms linked, laughing.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Joy inspects Honesty Island.
Oh, I love Honesty Island! And that's the truth!
INT. MINNESOTA HOUSE - DAY
A guilty Riley stands by a broken plate. HAMMER from behind her back. She reveals a
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Joy examines Family Island.
And, of course, Family Island is
EXT. MINNESOTA KITCHEN TABLE - DAY
The family decorate cookies together. Riley stuffs one into Dad's mouth.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
The Emotions look out over the Islands of Personality.
The point is, the Islands of Personality are what make Riley... Riley!
INT. BATHROOM
Riley splashes in the bathtub.
Look out, mermaid!
INSIDE OUT INT. MINNESOTA LIVING ROOM
Riley climbs the couch. She looks at the floor.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Joy projects an IMAGINATION of LAVA onto the screen.
INT. MINNESOTA LIVING ROOM
Lava!
YOUNG RILEY
Riley jumps from couch to chair to avoid the lava.
INT. MINNESOTA HOUSE
Riley draws. Pull back to reveal she's drawing on the wall.
(singing) Who's your friend who likes to play? Bing Bong, Bing Bong...
EXT. MINNESOTA LAKE - DAY
Six-year-old Riley skates across the ice backwards.
Hey! Very nice! Where did you learn that?
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Joy skates around the room, in sync with Riley.
EXT. MINNESOTA FRONT STEPS - DAY
Riley and Meg slurp frozen drinks. Riley grimaces: ice headache!
INT. HEADQUARTERS
EXT. MINNESOTA LAKE - DAY
A kids' hockey game. Riley takes a shot and scores!
INT. HEADQUARTERS JOY INT. RILEY'S MINNESOTA BEDROOM - NIGHT
Parents tuck Riley into bed and turn off the light.
Good night, kiddo!
G'night, Dad.
She looks up at the stars on her ceiling and falls asleep.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Joy watches the screen go DARK. She inspects the shelves mostly populated with GOLDEN MEMORIES.
And... we're out. That's what I'm talking about: another perfect day! Nice job everybody! Let's get those memories down to Long Term.
Joy pulls a lever. Memories cascade down the shelves.
All right, we did not die today! I call that an unqualified success.
The memories pachinko down the shelves and up a tube. Joy runs to the back window to watch them glide through the distant tubes towards Long-Term Memory like falling stars.
And that's it! We love our girl. She's got great friends and a great house. Things couldn't be better.
After all, Riley's 11 now. What
EXT. MINNESOTA HOUSE FRONT YARD - DAY
Moving sign: Sold!
SADNESS/FEAR/ANGER/DISGUST AIIIIIGHH!!!
EXT. DRIVEWAY - DAY
SLAM! A packed car hatchback closes.
Huh?
SADNESS/FEAR/ANGER/DISGUST AIIIIIGHH!!!
EXT. MINNESOTA STREET - DAY
VROOM! A moving van drives away.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
OK, not what I had in mind. SADNESS/FEAR/ANGER/DISGUST AIIIIIGHH!!!
EXT. AMERICAN HIGHWAYS

A SERIES OF SHOTS: the family car drives across America. car passes corn fields and rolling hills. TITLE CARD: A PIXAR ANIMATION STUDIOS FILM Riley sleeps in the back seat. The car zips around a mountain curve, down a desert highway. Riley looks out the back window.
INSIDE OUT
The car emerges from a tunnel onto the GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE. TITLE CARD: INSIDE OUT The car drives across the bridge. Riley looks out the window.
Hey look! The Golden Gate Bridge! Isn't that great?! It's not made out of solid gold like we thought, which is kind of a disappointment, but still!
The car drives past the Ferry Building.
I sure am glad you told me earthquakes are a myth, Joy. Otherwise I'd be terrified right now!
Everyone eyes each other behind Fear's back.
Uh... yeah.
EXT. LOMBARD STREET - DAY
The car is stuck in traffic. Cars HONK and people YELL.
These are my kind of people!
EXT. MARKET STREET - DAY
All right, just a few more blocks. We're almost to our new house!
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Step on it, Daddy!
Why don't we just live in this smelly car? We've already been in
it forever.
Which, actually, was really lucky, because that gave us plenty of time to think about what our new house is going to look like! Let's review the top five daydreams.
Joy plugs in DAYDREAMS showing fantasy houses: tree house,
water-slide house, etc.
Ooh! That looks safe!
Ohh, this is will be great for Riley! Oh, no, no, no, THIS one. A gingerbread house. DISGUST (O.S.) Ugh, Joy. For the last time, she cannot live in a cookie.
THAT'S the one! It comes with a dragon!
EXT. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSE - DAY
Now we're getting close, I can feel it. Here it is, here's our new house... and...
The car stops and Riley steps out. REVEAL: a run-down Victorian. Nothing like the daydreams.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Everyone looks slackjawed.
Maybe it's nice on the inside.
INSIDE OUT INT. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
Riley steps inside. The room is dark, dusty, uninviting.
We're supposed to live here?
Do we have to?
I'm telling you, it smells like something died in here.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
A disgust memory rolls in.
Can you die from moving?
Guys, you're overreacting. Nobody is dying-DISGUST
A DEAD MOUSE!!! ON THE SCREEN: A dead mouse in the corner.
Great, this is just great.
Ahhhhh!! It's the house of the dead! What are we gonna do?! We're gonna get rabies!!!
Fear jumps into Anger's arms. Anger fires up the flames.
GET OFFA ME!!!
Fear runs around on fire. Joy puts him out with a fire extinguisher.
Hey, hey, hey, all through the drive Dad talked about how cool our new room is. Let's go check it out!
FEAR/ANGER/DISGUST (O.S.) You're right, Joy/Yeah!/That's right!/Yes, yes, yes!
INT. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSE
Riley dashes up the stairs. INT. RILEY'S ROOM - CONTINUOUS It's tiny. Depressing. FEAR/DISGUST/ANGER (O.S.)
Oh, Riley can't live here. She's right.
It's the worst. It's absolutely the worst.
Really bad. FEAR
This house stinks.
Memories roll in: anger, disgust, fear.
Hey, it's nothing our butterfly curtains couldn't fix. I read
somewhere that an empty room is an opportunity.
Where did you read that?
It doesn't matter. I read it and it's great. We'll put the bed there. And the desk over there...
Joy projects an IMAGINATION of Riley's furniture. FEAR The hockey lamp goes there...
Put the chair there.
Posters... SADNESS
Well, the trophy collection goes there.
Stars! I like that.
Now we're talking! Let's go get our stuff from the moving van!
INT. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSE - DAY
Riley dashes down the stairs as Mom and Dad enter the house. Dad is on the phone.
Alright. Goodbye. (hangs up; to Mom) Well, guess what? The moving van won't be here until Thursday.
You're kidding.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
The van is lost?! This is the worst day ever!
ON THE SCREEN: Mom and Dad ARGUE.
You said it would be here yesterday!
I know that's what I said. That's what they told me!
Mom and Dad are stressed out!
FEAR/ANGER/DISGUST/SADNESS
Joy runs to storage and grabs what looks to be a LIGHTBULB.
I've got a great idea! She clicks the IDEA into the console.
INSIDE OUT INT. SAN FRANCISCO LIVING ROOM - DAY
Riley's face lights up. Riley grabs her hockey stick. Puts a wad of paper in play.
Did you even read the contract?
Honey, you act like this is my fault--
Andersen makes her move. She's
(grabbing a broom) Hey! Oh, no you're not!
She's lining up for the shot!
Coming behind you! Watch out!
She slides past him. Knocks the "puck" into the fireplace.
She shoots and she scores! Yeah!
INT. HEADQUARTERS
The Emotions cheer. Out the back window, Family Island runs.
INT. SAN FRANCISCO LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
(taunting Mom) Come on, Grandma!
Ha! "Grandma?"
Uh-oh, she put her hair up, we're in for it!
INT. SAN FRANCISCO LIVING ROOM
They all play hockey, Mom using a pillow to guard the "goal." Dad picks her up to shoot past her.
(laughing) Woo! Hey, put me down!
Dad scoops up Riley, too. Riley giggles.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
A happy memory rolls in. Joy's back in charge.
INT. SAN FRANCISCO LIVING ROOM
Dad's cell phone beeps.
Ugh. Sorry, hold on, hold on. (answers) Hello?
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Wait. Wha--?
INT. SAN FRANCISCO LIVING ROOM
You're kidding. All right. Stall for me, I'll be right there. (hangs up) The investor's supposed to show up on Thursday, not today! I gotta go.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Joy is disappointed.
INT. SAN FRANCISCO LIVING ROOM
It's okay. We get it.
You're the best. Thanks, Hon. (to Riley, as he leaves) See you, Sweetie.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Dad just left us.
Oh, he doesn't love us anymore. That's sad. I should drive, right?
JOY BLOCKS HER.
Joy? What are you doing?
Uh, just uh, gimme one second... You know what I've realized? Riley hasn't had lunch! (takes a memory off the wall and plays it) Remember?
MEMORY: A PIZZA SHOP. INT. SAN FRANCISCO LIVING ROOM
Hey I saw a pizza place down the street. Maybe we could try that?
Pizza sounds delicious!
INT. HEADQUARTERS
FEAR/ANGER/DISGUST
INT. PIZZA PLACE - DAY
Riley and Mom get their pizza. On it: broccoli.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
FEAR/JOY/DISGUST What the HECK IS THAT?!/Who puts
Congratulations, San Francisco, you've ruined pizza! First the Hawaiians, and now YOU!
EXT. SAN FRANCISCO STREET - LATER
Mom and Riley walk home. Riley looks disappointed.
What kind of a pizza place only serves one kind of pizza? Must be a San Francisco thing, huh?
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Joy glances at the wall of multi-colored memories. She sighs: things are not going well.
EXT. SAN FRANCISCO STREET - CONTINUOUS
Still, it's not as bad as the soup at that diner in Nebraska.
Oh yeah. The spoon stood up in the soup by itself! That was disgusting.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Joy looks out the back window. Family Island is going.
Oh good. Family is running.
EXT. SAN FRANCISCO STREET
The drive out was pretty fun, huh? What was your favorite part?
INSIDE OUT INT. HEADQUARTERS
Spitting out the car window!
Definitely not when Dad was singing.
Wearing a seat belt!
What about the time with the dinosaur!
Joy pulls up a memory. SADNESS/FEAR/ANGER/DISGUST Yeah./Yup./That's the one. ON THE SCREEN: Riley and Mom pose in front of a roadside cement dinosaur in Vernal, Utah.
Say cheese!
Behind Dad, the car rolls down the hill. No brakes!
Dad! Dad! Look behind you.
Honey. The car! The car!
Hold still. Huh? (runs off after the car) Stoooop! No no no nononono!!
Stegosaurus tail through the back window. The family laughs. Enjoying the memory, Joy and the others chuckle.
Nice one, Joy.
EXT. SAN FRANCISCO STREET
(smiling) I liked that time at the dinosaur. That was pretty funny.
INSIDE OUT INT. HEADQUARTERS
Joy's cheer is cut short when the dinosaur memory TURNS BLUE.
EXT. SAN FRANCISCO STREET - DAY
Riley's smile fades.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Wait. What? What happened?
Joy looks back. Sadness is touching the memory.
She did something to the memory.
Joy tries to fix the memory.
What did you do?
I just touched it.
That shouldn't make it change.
Change it back, Joy!
Joy rubs it, but the memory stays blue.
I'm trying.
You can't change it back?
No, I guess I can't!
Good going Sadness. Now when Riley thinks of that moment with Dad, she's gonna feel sad. Bravo.
I'm sorry Joy... I don't really know-- I thought maybe, if you-- if I-- if... I mean...
INSIDE OUT
Joy, we've got a stairway coming up.
EXT. SAN FRANCISCO STREET - DAY
Riley runs towards a stairway and handrail.
Just don't touch any other memories until we figure out what's going on.
(stepping up to controls) Alright. Get ready, this is a monster railing, and we are riding it all the way down!
EXT. SAN FRANCISCO STREET - CONTINUOUS
Riley sits on the railing, ready to slide.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Joy looks back: GOOFBALL ISLAND is whirring like crazy.
EXT. SAN FRANCISCO STREET
Riley goes to slide... but hops off and walks down instead.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Wait, what? What happened?
Suddenly a CORE MEMORY ROLLS up to Joy's feet.
Oh no!
INSIDE OUT
Out the back window, GOOFBALL ISLAND GOES DARK. Joy rushes to the Core Memory Holder.
Sadness! What are you doing?
It looked like one was crooked so I opened it and then it fell out!
Joy pops the memory back in. GOOFBALL ISLAND comes back on.
EXT. SAN FRANCISCO STREET
Riley jumps back on the railing and slides down. Woo hoo!
It's just that... I wanted to maybe hold one.
She reaches out to touch one, AND IT STARTS TO GO BLUE. JOY!
Joy pushes Sadness away before she can touch it.
Whoa, whoa, whoa!
The core memory TURNS BACK TO YELLOW.
Sadness! You nearly touched a core memory. And when you touch them, we can't change them back!
I know. I'm sorry. Something's wrong with me. It's like I'm having a breakdown.
You are not having a breakdown. It's stress.
I keep making mistakes like that. I'm awful...
Nooo, you're not.
...and annoying.
Well... uh... You know what? You can't focus on what's going wrong. There's always a way to turn things around, to find the fun!
Yeah. Find the fun. I don't know how to do that.
Okay. Well, try think of something funny!
Long pause.
Oh! Remember the funny movie where
the dog dies?
Yeah, that's not... (tries another approach) What about that time with Meg, when Riley laughed so hard milk came out
of her nose? I mean come on... INSERT: Riley spitting milk out of her nose, Meg laughing.
Yeah, that hurt. It felt like fire.
Okay, okay, don't think of that. Let's try something else. What are your favorite things to do?
My favorite? Um, well, I like it when we're outside.
That's good! Like there's the beach and sunshine... Oh! JOY (CONT'D) Like that time we buried Dad in the sand up to his neck? INSERT: Riley at beach, Dad buried in sand.
I was thinking more like rain.
INSERT: Riley stands in a downpour.
Rain? Rain... is my favorite too! We can stomp around in puddles...
INSERT: Riley happily jumps through puddles.
There's cool umbrellas, lightning storms...
More like when the rain runs down our back and makes our shoes soggy.
And we get all cold and shivery... INSERT: Riley's shoes fill with water. She looks miserable.
...and everything just starts feeling droopy... Sadness melts to the floor, crying.
Oh, hey, hey... easy. Why are you crying? That's really the opposite of what we're going for here.
Crying helps me slow down and obsess over the weight of life's problems.
Ugh, you know what? Let's think about something else. How about we read some mind manuals, huh? Sounds fun!
She drags Sadness over to a shelf of technical manuals.
I've read most of them.
Well have you read this one? This seems interesting: "Long-Term Memory Retrieval, Volume 47?" No.
Ohhh! A real page turner!
(opens manual; reads) "Long-Term Memory Data Selection
via channel sub-grouping?"
See? Fun already! You lucky dog, you're reading these cool things and I gotta go work. Life is so unfair.
Joy walks away. But just as she thinks things are handled... DING! Another memory -- Disgust -- slides in. Joy groans.
INT. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSE - NIGHT
Riley hops down the stairs towards the living room.
(on phone) What can we do? We've only got capital to last a month, maybe two. If we can't find investors by then, we're going to have to lay people off.
Riley hears the stress in Dad's voice.
Mom! Dad! Come kiss me g'night!
Be right there!
(on phone) I know, I know! We've got to land this, okay?
Riley heads back upstairs.
INSIDE OUT INT. RILEY'S ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
Riley climbs into her sleeping bag.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Did you hear Dad? He sounded really upset.
INT. RILEY'S ROOM
STRANGE NOISES from outside. Headlights cast shadows on the wall. Riley looks scared.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
What was that? Was it a bear? It's a bear!
There are no bears in San
I saw a really hairy guy. He looked
like a bear.
Oh, I'm so jumpy, my nerves are shot!
Ew, I don't want to hear about your nerves!
I'll tell you what it is. This move has been a bust.
That's what I've been telling you guys! There are at least 37 things for Riley to be scared of right now!
The smell alone is enough to make
her gag.
I can't believe Mom and Dad moved us here!
Look, I get it. You guys have concerns. But we've been through worse! Tell you what: let's make a list of all the things Riley should be HAPPY about!
Fine. Let's see... this house stinks, our room stinks...
Pizza is weird here...
Our friends are back home...
And all of our stuff is in the missing van!
Oh c'mon, it could be worse...
Yeah, Joy. We could be lying on the
dirty floor. In a bag.
INT. RILEY'S ROOM
Riley is lying on the dirty floor in a sleeping bag.
Okay, I admit it, we had a rough start. But think of all the good things that-ANGER No, Joy. There's absolutely no reason for Riley to be happy right now. Let us handle this.
I say we skip school tomorrow and lock ourselves in the bedroom.
We have no clean clothes. I mean, no one should see us.
Yeah, we could cry until we can't breathe.
We should lock the door and scream that curse word we know. It's a good one!
Now hold on! Look, we all have our
off days. You know, I-ON THE SCREEN: Mom opens Riley's bedroom door. Hi honey.
The Mom Bad News Train is pulling in! Toot toot!
INT. RILEY'S ROOM
Mom sits next to Riley.
Still no moving van. Now they're saying it won't be here 'til Tuesday, can you believe it?
INT. HEADQUARTERS
Toot toot toot!
INT. RILEY'S ROOM
Where's Dad?
On the phone. This new venture is keeping him pretty busy. Your dad's a little stressed -- you know, about getting his new company up and running...
I rest my case!
Joy sighs. She falls back from the controls. to the console. Anger steps up
Now for a few well-placed withering
scowls. INT. RILEY'S ROOM
I guess all I really want to say
is: thank you.
Huh?
Anger drops the controls. INT. RILEY'S ROOM
You know, through all this confusion you've stayed... well, you've stayed our happy girl!
Mom brushes Riley's cheek.
Your dad's under a lot of pressure. But if you and I can keep smiling, it would be a big help. We can do that for him, right?
Joy, smiling with renewed purpose, steps back to the console.
Whoa! Well.
INT. RILEY'S ROOM
(smiles) Yeah! Sure.
What did we do to deserve you? (kisses Riley) Sweet dreams.
Good night.
Well, you can't argue with Mom. "Happy" it is.
Sounds great!
I'm totally behind you, Joy.
INT. RILEY'S ROOM
Riley goes to sleep. The screen goes black.
Looks like we're going into REM. I got Dream Duty, so I'll take care of sending these to Long Term. Great day today, guys! Sleep well
The team heads off to bed. Harp music plays.
Alright, what's on tonight, Dream Production?
ON THE SCREEN: a DREAM COMES UP. The family FLIES happily through the air in the car. They land in front of the house--
INSIDE OUT
Well, this is it. The new place.
-- except it's haunted! Organ music. Ghosts howling. The dead mouse rises up into frame.
Come live with me, Riley!
The mouse falls over, dead. A bear enters, holding a pizza.
Somebody order a broccoli pizza?
The pizza slices hop up and dance a jig.
Eat me! I'm organic!
INT. RILEY'S ROOM
Riley frowns in her sleep.
Ah, NO, who is in charge of programming down there?! I know I'm not supposed to do this, but...
Joy ducks behind the console and REBOOTS it. The dream ends.
INT. RILEY'S ROOM - NIGHT
Riley still looks troubled.
INT. HEADQUARTERS
We are not going to end the day like this.
Joy has a thought.
She recalls a memory.
ON THE SCREEN: Riley ice skates with her parents. Joy watches, loving this kid. She shadows Riley, "skating" around headquarters.
JOY (CONT'D) Don't you worry. I'm gonna make sure that tomorrow is another great day. I promise.
INT. RILEY'S ROOM - NIGHT
Riley SMILES in her sleep.
INT. KITCHEN - MORNING
Riley excitedly snarfs down breakfast cereal.
INT. HEADQUARTERS - MORNING
Joy plays the accordion around Headquarters. The others appear, looking grumpy. Joy yells over the noise.
Hello! Did I wake you?
DO you have to play that?
Well, I have to practice. And I don't think of it as playing so much as hugging.
She tosses aside the accordion and runs off.
Okay, first day of school! Very, very exciting! I was up late last night figuring out a new plan. Here it is. (to Fear) Fear! I need a list of all the possible negative outcomes on the
first day at a new school.
Way ahead of you there. Does anyone know how to spell "meteor?"
Disgust! Make sure Riley stands out today... but also blends in.
When I'm through, Riley will look
